hero.question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd has essentially written Steyer's 2026 gubernatorial run off at 12.4%, pricing him as a long-shot donor with name recognition but zero actual political infrastructure or path to a primary victory. Watch for the post-2024 cycle realignment: if Newsom consolidates power and the field stays fragmented between Shapiro-aligned moderates and the progressive lane, Steyer's $50M+ war chest suddenly becomes relevant again, which would make this 12-13% dot a steal.

daily_take.headline: Steyer's California Comeback: Market Pricing in a 2026 Dark Horse
daily_take.deck: Tom Steyer just jumped 4.2 points to 12.4% in 24 hours on $2M volume, signaling real money thinks he's got a lane in California's 2026 governor race. The move feels overcooked. A billionaire with name recognition and deep pockets gets a floor, but Steyer's 2020 and 2024 presidential failures suggest he can't close. Fade the enthusiasm and wait for 8-10% before considering a position.
